members.name: ClearTilesOutsideTexture
members.summary: Removes all tiles that don't fit the available texture area. This method iterates over all the source's tiles, so it's advised to use [method has_tiles_outside_texture] beforehand.

members.name: CreateAlternativeTile
members.signature: public int CreateAlternativeTile(Vector2i atlasCoords, int alternativeIdOverride)
members.summary: Creates an alternative tile for the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords]. If [param alternative_id_override] is -1, give it an automatically generated unique ID, or assigns it the given ID otherwise. Returns the new alternative identifier, or -1 if the alternative could not be created with a provided [param alternative_id_override].

members.name: CreateTile
members.signature: public void CreateTile(Vector2i atlasCoords, Vector2i size)
members.summary: Creates a new tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords] with the given [param size].

members.name: GetAtlasGridSize
members.summary: Returns the atlas grid size, which depends on how many tiles can fit in the texture. It thus depends on the [member texture]'s size, the atlas [member margins], and the tiles' [member texture_region_size].

members.name: GetNextAlternativeTileId
members.summary: Returns the alternative ID a following call to [method create_alternative_tile] would return.

members.name: GetRuntimeTexture
members.summary: If [member use_texture_padding] is [code]false[/code], returns [member texture]. Otherwise, returns an internal [ImageTexture] created that includes the padding.

members.name: GetRuntimeTileTextureRegion
members.signature: public Rect2i GetRuntimeTileTextureRegion(Vector2i atlasCoords, int frame)
members.summary: Returns the region of the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords] for the given [param frame] inside the texture returned by [method get_runtime_texture]. [b]Note:[/b] If [member use_texture_padding] is [code]false[/code], returns the same as [method get_tile_texture_region].

members.name: GetTileAnimationColumns
members.summary: Returns how many columns the tile at [param atlas_coords] has in its animation layout.

members.name: GetTileAnimationFrameDuration
members.signature: public float GetTileAnimationFrameDuration(Vector2i atlasCoords, int frameIndex)
members.summary: Returns the animation frame duration of frame [param frame_index] for the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords].

members.name: GetTileAnimationFramesCount
members.summary: Returns how many animation frames has the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords].

members.name: GetTileAnimationMode
members.summary: Returns the tile animation mode of the tile at [param atlas_coords]. See also [method set_tile_animation_mode].

members.name: GetTileAnimationSeparation
members.summary: Returns the separation (as in the atlas grid) between each frame of an animated tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords].

members.name: GetTileAnimationSpeed
members.summary: Returns the animation speed of the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords].

members.name: GetTileAnimationTotalDuration
members.summary: Returns the sum of the sum of the frame durations of the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords]. This value needs to be divided by the animation speed to get the actual animation loop duration.

members.name: GetTileAtCoords
members.summary: If there is a tile covering the [param atlas_coords] coordinates, returns the top-left coordinates of the tile (thus its coordinate ID). Returns [code]Vector2i(-1, -1)[/code] otherwise.

members.name: GetTileData
members.signature: public TileData GetTileData(Vector2i atlasCoords, int alternativeTile)
members.summary: Returns the [TileData] object for the given atlas coordinates and alternative ID.

members.name: GetTileSizeInAtlas
members.summary: Returns the size of the tile (in the grid coordinates system) at coordinates [param atlas_coords].

members.name: GetTileTextureRegion
members.signature: public Rect2i GetTileTextureRegion(Vector2i atlasCoords, int frame)
members.summary: Returns a tile's texture region in the atlas texture. For animated tiles, a [param frame] argument might be provided for the different frames of the animation.

members.name: GetTilesToBeRemovedOnChange
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array GetTilesToBeRemovedOnChange(Texture2D texture, Vector2i margins, Vector2i separation, Vector2i textureRegionSize)
members.summary: Returns an array of tiles coordinates ID that will be automatically removed when modifying one or several of those properties: [param texture], [param margins], [param separation] or [param texture_region_size]. This can be used to undo changes that would have caused tiles data loss.

members.name: HasRoomForTile
members.signature: public bool HasRoomForTile(Vector2i atlasCoords, Vector2i size, int animationColumns, Vector2i animationSeparation, int framesCount, Vector2i ignoredTile)
members.summary: Returns whether there is enough room in an atlas to create/modify a tile with the given properties. If [param ignored_tile] is provided, act as is the given tile was not present in the atlas. This may be used when you want to modify a tile's properties.

members.name: HasTilesOutsideTexture
members.summary: Checks if the source has any tiles that don't fit the texture area (either partially or completely).

members.name: MoveTileInAtlas
members.signature: public void MoveTileInAtlas(Vector2i atlasCoords, Vector2i newAtlasCoords, Vector2i newSize)
members.summary: Move the tile and its alternatives at the [param atlas_coords] coordinates to the [param new_atlas_coords] coordinates with the [param new_size] size. This functions will fail if a tile is already present in the given area. If [param new_atlas_coords] is [code]Vector2i(-1, -1)[/code], keeps the tile's coordinates. If [param new_size] is [code]Vector2i(-1, -1)[/code], keeps the tile's size. To avoid an error, first check if a move is possible using [method has_room_for_tile].

members.name: RemoveAlternativeTile
members.signature: public void RemoveAlternativeTile(Vector2i atlasCoords, int alternativeTile)
members.summary: Remove a tile's alternative with alternative ID [param alternative_tile]. Calling this function with [param alternative_tile] equals to 0 will fail, as the base tile alternative cannot be removed.

members.name: RemoveTile
members.summary: Remove a tile and its alternative at coordinates [param atlas_coords].

members.name: SetAlternativeTileId
members.signature: public void SetAlternativeTileId(Vector2i atlasCoords, int alternativeTile, int newId)
members.summary: Change a tile's alternative ID from [param alternative_tile] to [param new_id]. Calling this function with [param new_id] of 0 will fail, as the base tile alternative cannot be moved.

members.name: SetTileAnimationColumns
members.signature: public void SetTileAnimationColumns(Vector2i atlasCoords, int frameColumns)
members.summary: Sets the number of columns in the animation layout of the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords]. If set to 0, then the different frames of the animation are laid out as a single horizontal line in the atlas.

members.name: SetTileAnimationFrameDuration
members.signature: public void SetTileAnimationFrameDuration(Vector2i atlasCoords, int frameIndex, float duration)
members.summary: Sets the animation frame [param duration] of frame [param frame_index] for the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords].

members.name: SetTileAnimationFramesCount
members.signature: public void SetTileAnimationFramesCount(Vector2i atlasCoords, int framesCount)
members.summary: Sets how many animation frames the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords] has.

members.name: SetTileAnimationMode
members.signature: public void SetTileAnimationMode(Vector2i atlasCoords, int mode)
members.summary: Sets the tile animation mode of the tile at [param atlas_coords] to [param mode]. See also [method get_tile_animation_mode].

members.name: SetTileAnimationSeparation
members.signature: public void SetTileAnimationSeparation(Vector2i atlasCoords, Vector2i separation)
members.summary: Sets the margin (in grid tiles) between each tile in the animation layout of the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords] has.

members.name: SetTileAnimationSpeed
members.signature: public void SetTileAnimationSpeed(Vector2i atlasCoords, float speed)
members.summary: Sets the animation speed of the tile at coordinates [param atlas_coords] has.

members.name: Margins
members.summary: Margins, in pixels, to offset the origin of the grid in the texture.

members.name: Separation
members.summary: Separation, in pixels, between each tile texture region of the grid.

members.name: Texture
members.summary: The atlas texture.

members.name: TextureRegionSize
members.summary: The base tile size in the texture (in pixel). This size must be bigger than or equal to the TileSet's [code]tile_size[/code] value.

members.name: UseTexturePadding
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], generates an internal texture with an additional one pixel padding around each tile. Texture padding avoids a common artifact where lines appear between tiles. Disabling this setting might lead a small performance improvement, as generating the internal texture requires both memory and processing time when the TileSetAtlasSource resource is modified.

values.name: TILE_ANIMATION_MODE_DEFAULT
values.summary: Tile animations start at same time, looking identical.

values.name: TILE_ANIMATION_MODE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TileAnimationMode] enum.

values.name: TILE_ANIMATION_MODE_RANDOM_START_TIMES
values.summary: Tile animations start at random times, looking varied.

constants.name: TILE_ANIMATION_MODE_DEFAULT
constants.summary: Tile animations start at same time, looking identical.

constants.name: TILE_ANIMATION_MODE_RANDOM_START_TIMES
constants.summary: Tile animations start at random times, looking varied.

constants.name: TILE_ANIMATION_MODE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TileAnimationMode] enum.

constants.name: TRANSFORM_FLIP_H
constants.summary: Represents cell's horizontal flip flag. Should be used directly with [TileMapLayer] to flip placed tiles by altering their alternative IDs. [codeblock] var alternate_id = $TileMapLayer.get_cell_alternative_tile(Vector2i(2, 2)) if not alternate_id & TileSetAtlasSource.TRANSFORM_FLIP_H: # If tile is not already flipped, flip it. $TileMapLayer.set_cell(Vector2i(2, 2), source_id, atlas_coords, alternate_id | TileSetAtlasSource.TRANSFORM_FLIP_H) [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] These transformations can be combined to do the equivalent of 0, 90, 180, and 270 degree rotations, as shown below: [codeblock] enum TileTransform { ROTATE_0 = 0, ROTATE_90 = TileSetAtlasSource.TRANSFORM_TRANSPOSE | TileSetAtlasSource.TRANSFORM_FLIP_H, ROTATE_180 = TileSetAtlasSource.TRANSFORM_FLIP_H | TileSetAtlasSource.TRANSFORM_FLIP_V, ROTATE_270 = TileSetAtlasSource.TRANSFORM_TRANSPOSE | TileSetAtlasSource.TRANSFORM_FLIP_V, } [/codeblock]

constants.name: TRANSFORM_FLIP_V
constants.summary: Represents cell's vertical flip flag. See [constant TRANSFORM_FLIP_H] for usage.

constants.name: TRANSFORM_TRANSPOSE
constants.summary: Represents cell's transposed flag. See [constant TRANSFORM_FLIP_H] for usage.
